questions.prompt: Your scheduler retries on a transient failure. Users sometimes receive the same morning brief twice. Fix?

choices.text: Add idempotency keys keyed by `{user_id}:{task}:{date}`. Before running, check if the key has been processed; if yes, skip. Storage of processed keys can be ephemeral (TTL after a day). Same key twice = skip. Standard pattern for any work that can be retried.

questions.explanation: Schedulers fire twice — system restarts, retries on transient failures, race conditions. Idempotency keys per occurrence make double-runs no-ops. The same pattern applies to any work-on-behalf-of-user system.

questions.prompt: What's non-negotiable in the recurring-task UX?

choices.text: Pause and disable controls — per-task pause, vacation mode (pause all), quiet hours, day-of-week overrides. Users need to be able to halt scheduled work instantly when life happens. Without it, retention drops every time someone goes on vacation or hits a busy week.

questions.explanation: Pause controls are some of the most-used assistant features once they exist. Treat them as first-class, not a v2 maybe. Per-task, vacation, quiet-hours, day-of-week — each catches a different real user need.
